achievements.name: Meganium, Typhlosion or Feraligatr
achievements.description: Evolve your starter to its third form.

achievements.name: Bellsprout
achievements.note: catch (any time) — get a second for the Onix trade

achievements.name: The Silver Case
achievements.description: Give your rival's name to the officer at Professor Elm's lab.
achievements.callout_do: Give your rival's name to the officer at Elm's lab.
howto: Story-related — happens automatically when you return from Mr. Pokémon.

achievements.name: Zephyr Badge
achievements.description: Defeat Falkner with all 46 possible Pokémon in the Pokédex.

achievements.name: Flying in Father's Footsteps
achievements.description: Defeat Falkner in Violet City and earn the Zephyr Badge.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat Falkner for the Zephyr Badge.

achievements.name: Clipping the Pigeon's Wings
achievements.description: Defeat Falkner in the Set battle style using only Johto Pokémon that do not exceed level 9 during battle and without using any Pack items - no external trade-only Pokémon.
achievements.callout_do: Optional challenge: beat Falkner in Set style, Johto-only, all Pokémon ≤ Lv. 9, no Pack items.
howto: If you chose Cyndaquil or Totodile this is easy. Otherwise a Dunsparce from Dark Cave with Rage (more damage each time it is hit) shreds the gym; it is Normal type so takes little damage.
Set the battle style to Set and remove items from your bag's usable pool for the fight.

achievements.name: Togepi
achievements.note: hatch the Egg (walk with it in your party)

achievements.name: But Which Came First?
achievements.description: Receive an Everstone from Professor Elm by showing him the hatched Mystery Egg.
achievements.callout_do: Show Elm your hatched Togepi for the Everstone.

achievements.name: Krabby
achievements.note: fish up (Cherrygrove) — get a second for the trade

achievements.name: Criminal Underground
achievements.description: Defeat the Rocket Grunt leading the raid on the Slowpoke Well.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat the Rocket Grunt leading the Slowpoke Well raid.

achievements.name: Apri-Cornucopia
achievements.description: Receive a batch of at least 10 special Poké Balls at once from Kurt in Azalea Town.
achievements.callout_do: Receive at least 10 special Poké Balls at once from Kurt.
howto: Hand Kurt a batch of Apricorns so he returns 10+ balls in one go.

achievements.name: I Slept Like a Log...
achievements.description: Encounter a sleeping Pokémon by using Headbutt on a tree.
achievements.callout_do: Headbutt a tree and run into a sleeping Pokémon.

achievements.name: Hive Badge
achievements.description: Defeat Bugsy with all 77 possible Pokémon in the Pokédex; Heracross is not required.

achievements.name: Entomology Prodigy
achievements.description: Defeat Bugsy in Azalea Town and earn the Hive Badge.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat Bugsy in Azalea Town for the Hive Badge.

achievements.name: Did Someone Call an Exterminator?
achievements.description: Defeat Bugsy in the Set battle style using only Johto Pokémon that do not exceed level 16 during battle and without using any Pack items - no external trade-only Pokémon.
achievements.callout_do: Optional challenge: beat Bugsy in Set style, Johto-only, ≤ Lv. 16, no Pack items.
howto: A Dunsparce with Rage still shreds this gym; Cyndaquil starters win outright. Hoothoot/Wooper also help.

achievements.name: Abra
achievements.description: Catch an Abra or receive one from the Game Corner.
achievements.note: catch (any time) — get a second for the Machop trade

achievements.name: Served Shiny Side Up?
achievements.description: Receive the Odd Egg from the Day-Care Man on Route 34.
achievements.callout_do: Receive the Odd Egg from the Day-Care Man on Route 34.

achievements.name: Tyrogue
achievements.description: Hatch a Tyrogue. (SAVE before you obtain the odd egg from the couple, read comments).
achievements.note: hatch into Tyrogue (save before receiving the egg)

achievements.name: Machop
achievements.note: trade your spare Abra in the Underground

achievements.name: Cubone
achievements.description: Obtain a Cubone from the Game Corner.

achievements.name: Wobbuffet
achievements.description: Obtain a Wobbuffet from the Game Corner.

achievements.name: Deals on Wheels
achievements.description: Promote the Miracle Cycle bike shop in Goldenrod City and be allowed to keep the bike.
achievements.callout_do: Promote the Miracle Cycle bike shop so you keep the bike.

achievements.name: Our Store's Up High, But Our Prices Are Low
achievements.description: Make a purchase in the Rooftop Sale on the roof of the Goldenrod Department Store.
achievements.callout_do: Make a purchase at the Goldenrod Dept. Store Rooftop Sale.

achievements.name: Gambler's Charity
achievements.description: Learn a new move from Bill's father outside the Goldenrod City Game Corner.
achievements.callout_do: Learn a move from Bill's father outside the Game Corner.

achievements.name: Password Perfectionist
achievements.description: Add Buena's phone number to your Pokégear after earning 30 Blue Card points.
achievements.callout_do: Earn 30 Blue Card points to add Buena's number to your PokéGear.
howto: Listen to Buena's Password on the radio, then answer at the Goldenrod radio desk for points each day.

achievements.name: Megaton Chances
achievements.description: See Golem by lining up two 7s on a slot machine at a Game Corner.
achievements.callout_do: Line up two 7s on a Game Corner slot machine to see Golem.

achievements.name: Underage Gambling Pays Off!
achievements.description: Earn a 7-7-7 on a slot machine at a Game Corner.
achievements.callout_do: Hit a 7-7-7 on a Game Corner slot machine.

achievements.name: Double Dozen Deduction
achievements.description: Win a x24 bet in the Card Flip minigame at a Game Corner.
achievements.callout_do: Win a x24 bet in the Game Corner Card Flip minigame.

achievements.name: Cast Your Net Far & Wide
achievements.description: Win the Bug-Catching Contest at the National Park.
achievements.callout_do: Win the Bug-Catching Contest.
howto: Save before entering so you can retry. Put a Pokémon to sleep and catch it at full HP for the best score; a Scyther or Pinsir is ideal.

achievements.name: Cuddly Crawlie Challenge
achievements.description: Win the Bug-Catching Contest without catching a Butterfree, a Beedrill, a Scyther, or a Pinsir.
achievements.callout_do: Win the contest WITHOUT catching Butterfree, Beedrill, Scyther or Pinsir.

achievements.name: Earning Style Points
achievements.description: Win the Bug-Catching Contest with Cooltrainer Nick as a participant.
achievements.callout_do: Win the contest with Cooltrainer Nick as a participant.

achievements.name: Element of Generosity
achievements.description: Receive an Evolution stone from a registered Pokégear Trainer.
achievements.callout_do: Receive an evolution stone from a registered PokéGear Trainer.
howto: Gina gives Leaf Stones, Alan gives Fire Stones, plus later contacts for Water/Thunder Stones.

achievements.name: Plain Badge
achievements.description: Defeat Whitney with all 109 possible Pokémon in the Pokédex.

achievements.name: No Crying over Spilt Milk
achievements.description: Defeat Whitney in Goldenrod City and earn the Plain Badge.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat Whitney in Goldenrod City for the Plain Badge.
howto: Her Miltank is infamous — Dunsparce with Rage or a Heracross handle it.

achievements.name: Attack of the Mad Cow
achievements.description: Defeat Whitney in the Set battle style using only Johto Pokémon that do not exceed level 20 during battle and without using any Pack items - no external trade-only Pokémon.
achievements.callout_do: Optional challenge: beat Whitney in Set style, Johto-only, ≤ Lv. 20, no Pack items.
howto: Lead with Dunsparce and spam Rage; even Miltank falls.

achievements.name: Falsa Wood
achievements.description: Catch the Sudowoodo on Route 36 and register it in the Pokédex.
achievements.callout_do: Catch the Sudowoodo on Route 36 and register it.

achievements.name: Sudowoodo
achievements.note: static battle — save before using the SquirtBottle

achievements.name: Jolteon
achievements.note: Thunder Stone → Jolteon (stones from Lass Dana, Route 38)

achievements.name: Vaporeon
achievements.note: Water Stone → Vaporeon (stones from Tully, Route 42)

achievements.name: Espeon
achievements.note: raise happiness during the DAY → Espeon

achievements.name: Umbreon
achievements.note: raise happiness during the NIGHT → Umbreon

achievements.name: Have Eusine What I've Seen?!
achievements.description: Complete the Eusine sidequest in the Burned Tower.
achievements.callout_do: Complete the Eusine sidequest in the Burned Tower.

achievements.name: Weezing
achievements.description: Evolve Koffing or catch a Weezing.
achievements.note: level to Lv. 35 → Weezing (or catch the rare 1% Weezing)

achievements.name: Magneton
achievements.note: level to Lv. 30 → Magneton (no Magnezone in Gen 2)

achievements.name: Udder Selflessness
achievements.description: Nurse Moomoo back to health at Moomoo Farm.
achievements.callout_do: Nurse Moomoo back to health at Moomoo Farm.

achievements.name: Shellder
achievements.note: Good Rod at Olivine harbor / New Bark Town

achievements.name: Chinchou
achievements.note: Good Rod at Olivine harbor / New Bark Town

achievements.name: Don't Give Them the Stick!
achievements.description: Obtain a Stick from a wild Farfetch'd by either catching the Pokémon or stealing it.
achievements.callout_do: Obtain a Stick by catching or Thief-ing a wild Farfetch'd.

achievements.name: Beasts of the Brass
achievements.description: Catch Entei and Raikou, the Volcano and Thunder Pokémon.
achievements.callout_do: Catch BOTH Entei and Raikou.

achievements.name: Lightning in a Ball
achievements.description: Catch Raikou and register it in the Pokédex without using a Master Ball.
achievements.callout_do: Catch Raikou without using a Master Ball.

achievements.name: Pyroplastic Pacifier
achievements.description: Catch Entei and register it in the Pokédex without using a Master Ball.
achievements.callout_do: Catch Entei without using a Master Ball.

achievements.name: Life, the Universe, and Aurorae
achievements.callout_do: Encounter Suicune on Route 42.

achievements.name: Fog Badge
achievements.description: Defeat Morty with all 142 possible Pokémon in the Pokédex.

achievements.name: The Future Is Fright
achievements.description: Defeat Morty in Ecruteak City and earn the Fog Badge.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat Morty in Ecruteak City for the Fog Badge.

achievements.name: In the Spirit of Battle
achievements.description: Defeat Morty in the Set battle style using only Johto Pokémon that do not exceed level 25 during battle and without using any Pack items - no external trade-only Pokémon.
achievements.callout_do: Optional challenge: beat Morty in Set style, Johto-only, ≤ Lv. 25, no Pack items.
howto: A Totodile line (Bite/Crunch) or a Crobat handles his Ghosts; his team often Curses itself to half HP.

achievements.name: Natu
achievements.note: catch (Ruins of Alph grass, any time)

achievements.name: Lapras
achievements.note: Union Cave B2F Surf, Fridays only (Lv. 20)

achievements.name: Ferry Fountain Fridays
achievements.description: Catch the Lapras in Union Cave and register it in the Pokédex.
achievements.callout_do: Catch the Union Cave Lapras and register it.

achievements.name: The Dashing Dog-Chaser
achievements.callout_do: Defeat Eusine in Cianwood City.

achievements.name: Worth Its Weight in Mold
achievements.description: Get Mania of Cianwood City's permission to keep Shuckie by raising its Friendship high enough.
achievements.callout_do: Raise Shuckie's Friendship high enough that Mania lets you keep it.

achievements.name: Sorry, No Rare Candies!
achievements.description: Have a Shuckle turn a Berry into Berry Juice after a battle.
achievements.callout_do: Have a Shuckle turn a Berry into Berry Juice after a battle.

achievements.name: Forest of Fate
achievements.callout_do: Encounter Suicune on Route 36.

achievements.name: Everybody Gets One
achievements.description: Catch the red Gyarados in the Lake of Rage.
achievements.callout_do: Catch the red Gyarados in the Lake of Rage.

achievements.name: Scaling for Scaling
achievements.description: Trade the Red Scale to Mr. Pokémon on Route 30 for the EXP Share.
achievements.callout_do: Trade the Red Scale (from the red Gyarados) to Mr. Pokémon for the EXP Share.

achievements.name: That Bass's Too Fat!
achievements.description: Receive an Elixir from the Fishing Guru at the Lake of Rage by breaking the Magikarp size record.
achievements.callout_do: Break the Magikarp size record for the Lake of Rage Guru's Elixir.

achievements.name: Evil Makes Waves
achievements.description: Thwart the efforts of Team Rocket at their hideout in Mahogany Town.
achievements.callout_do: Thwart Team Rocket at their Mahogany Town hideout.

achievements.name: Calling Off the Launch
achievements.description: Defeat every Trainer at Team Rocket's Hideout in Mahogany Town.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat EVERY Trainer at the Mahogany Rocket Hideout.

achievements.name: Glacier Badge
achievements.description: Defeat Pryce with all 150 possible Pokémon in the Pokédex.

achievements.name: Out with the Cold, In with the New
achievements.description: Defeat Pryce in Mahogany Town and earn the Glacier Badge.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat Pryce in Mahogany Town for the Glacier Badge.

achievements.name: Braving a Brutal Blizzard
achievements.description: Defeat Pryce in the Set battle style using only Johto Pokémon that do not exceed level 31 during battle and without using any Pack items - no external trade-only Pokémon.
achievements.callout_do: Optional challenge: beat Pryce in Set style, Johto-only, ≤ Lv. 31, no Pack items.
howto: Note the level cap is 31 here, LOWER than Jasmine's — don't over-level your ice answer. A Cyndaquil line or Quagsire shreds him.

achievements.name: Horsea
achievements.note: Surf the pools (Horsea 30%+, rising toward the inner cave)

achievements.name: Seadra
achievements.description: Catch a Seadra or evolve Horsea to Seadra.
achievements.note: level Horsea to Lv. 32, or catch a Seadra (Super Rod)

achievements.name: Working the Light Shift
achievements.description: Cure Amphy at the top of the Olivine Lighthouse.
achievements.callout_do: Cure Amphy at the top of the Olivine Lighthouse.

achievements.name: Bare-Fisted Beast Taming
achievements.description: Defeat Chuck in Cianwood City and earn the Storm Badge.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat Chuck in Cianwood City for the Storm Badge.

achievements.name: Black Belt in Training
achievements.description: Defeat Chuck in the Set battle style using only Johto Pokémon that do not exceed level 30 during battle and without using any Pack items - no external trade-only Pokémon.
achievements.callout_do: Optional challenge: beat Chuck in Set style, Johto-only, ≤ Lv. 30, no Pack items.
howto: A Chinchou/Lanturn, Togetic, Mantine or Crobat carries this Flying-weak gym.

achievements.name: Storm Badge
achievements.description: Defeat Chuck with all 154 possible Pokémon in the Pokédex.

achievements.name: Iron Maiden
achievements.description: Defeat Jasmine in Olivine City and earn the Mineral Badge.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat Jasmine in Olivine City for the Mineral Badge.

achievements.name: Stainless Stealing Victory
achievements.description: Defeat Jasmine in the Set battle style using only Johto Pokémon that do not exceed level 35 during battle and without using any Pack items - no external trade-only Pokémon.
achievements.callout_do: Optional challenge: beat Jasmine in Set style, Johto-only, ≤ Lv. 35, no Pack items.
howto: Wooper/Quagsire (Ground) ignore her Magnetons; Sudowoodo and Heracross bring Fighting moves.

achievements.name: Mineral Badge
achievements.description: Defeat Jasmine with all 154 possible Pokémon in the Pokédex.

achievements.name: Taken Off the Air
achievements.description: Defeat every Rocket Executive in the Goldenrod Radio Tower.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat every Rocket Executive in the Goldenrod Radio Tower.

achievements.name: Golden-Rocket Roundup
achievements.description: Defeat every Trainer in the Goldenrod Radio Tower and the Underground.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat EVERY Trainer in the Radio Tower and the Underground.

achievements.name: Calming the Northern Winds
achievements.callout_do: Catch Suicune, the Aurora Pokémon.

achievements.name: Crystalline Catch
achievements.description: Catch Suicune and register it in the Pokédex without using a Master Ball.
achievements.callout_do: Catch Suicune and register it without using a Master Ball.

achievements.name: Suicune
achievements.note: catch at the Tin Tower (will not flee)

achievements.name: Dratini
achievements.note: fish the southern pond (Good/Super Rod); get one female

achievements.name: Dodrio
achievements.note: trade a female Dragonair for Dodrio in Blackthorn

achievements.name: Doduo
achievements.note: breed Dodrio × Ditto, then hatch Doduo

achievements.name: Smoochum
achievements.note: breed Jynx × Ditto, then hatch Smoochum

achievements.name: Rising Badge
achievements.description: Obtain the badge from Clair with all 170 possible Pokémon in the Pokédex.

achievements.name: Dragon's Destiny
achievements.description: Defeat Clair in Blackthorn City and earn the Rising Badge.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat Clair in Blackthorn City for the Rising Badge.

achievements.name: Down to the Last Breath
achievements.description: Defeat Clair in the Set battle style using only Johto Pokémon that do not exceed level 40 during battle and without using any Pack items - no external trade-only Pokémon or Legendaries other than the Beasts.
achievements.callout_do: Optional challenge: beat Clair in Set style, Johto-only, ≤ Lv. 40, no Pack items (Beasts allowed).
howto: Swinub/Piloswine (Ice) is Clair's nightmare; Skarmory resists her Dragons. Crobat and Lanturn also shine.

achievements.name: Truthfulness Bears Rewards
achievements.description: Receive a Dratini with ExtremeSpeed at the Dragon Shrine by correctly answering every question of the Master's quiz on the first attempt.
achievements.callout_do: Answer the Dragon Shrine quiz perfectly for an ExtremeSpeed Dratini.

achievements.name: Rhydon
achievements.description: Catch a Rhydon or evolve Rhyhorn to Rhydon.
achievements.note: evolve at Lv. 42 (or catch a wild Rhydon, 5%)

achievements.name: The Thief's Last Stand
achievements.description: Win the optional battle against your rival at Indigo Plateau.
achievements.callout_do: Win the optional rival battle at the Indigo Plateau.

achievements.name: Elite Four
achievements.description: Defeat the Elite Four with all 174 possible Pokémon in the Pokédex.

achievements.name: The Johto Journey's End
achievements.description: Defeat the Elite Four and Champion Lance at the Pokémon League, and be crowned as the new Pokémon League Champion!
achievements.callout_do: Defeat the Elite Four and Champion Lance to become Champion!

achievements.name: Overcoming the Heavenly Kings
achievements.description: Defeat the Elite Four and Lance in a single session in the Set battle style using only Johto Pokémon that do not exceed level 44 during battle and without using any Pack items - no external trade-only Pokémon or Legendaries other than the Beasts.
achievements.callout_do: Optional challenge: beat the Elite Four in Set style, Johto-only, ≤ Lv. 44, no Pack items, one session (Beasts allowed).
howto: Swinub/Piloswine and Skarmory are excellent vs Lance; Suicune, Crobat, Lanturn and Quagsire round out a strong six.

achievements.name: Struggle to the Top
achievements.description: Defeat the Elite Four and Lance using only a single Pokémon.
achievements.callout_do: Optional challenge: defeat the Elite Four and Lance using only a single Pokémon.

achievements.name: Cruisin' for a Bruisin'
achievements.description: Defeat every possible Trainer aboard the S.S. Aqua excluding those from the first voyage.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat every Trainer aboard the S.S. Aqua (after the first voyage).

achievements.name: We're Gonna Give You the Power
achievements.description: Return the stolen Machine Part to the staff of the Kanto Power Plant.
achievements.callout_do: Return the stolen Machine Part to the Power Plant staff.

achievements.name: More Like the Orphan Club!
achievements.description: Obtain a Thick Club from a wild Cubone or Marowak.
achievements.callout_do: Obtain a Thick Club from a wild Cubone or Marowak.

achievements.name: This One's For You, Doll
achievements.description: Receive a Pass for the Magnet Train from the Copycat in Saffron City.
achievements.callout_do: Receive the Magnet Train Pass from the Saffron Copycat.

achievements.name: Muk
achievements.description: Catch a Muk or evolve Grimer to Muk.
achievements.note: catch via Surf (10%) or evolve Grimer at Lv. 38

achievements.name: Porygon
achievements.description: Obtain a Porygon at the Game Corner.
achievements.note: buy at the Celadon Game Corner (5,555 coins)

achievements.name: Larvitar
achievements.description: Obtain a Larvitar at the Game Corner.
achievements.note: buy at the Celadon Game Corner (8,888 coins) — arrives Lv. 40

achievements.name: Easter Surprise
achievements.description: Obtain a Lucky Egg from a wild Chansey by either catching the Pokémon or stealing it.
achievements.callout_do: Obtain a Lucky Egg by catching or Thief-ing a wild Chansey.

achievements.name: Aerodactyl
achievements.note: trade a Chansey for Aerodactyl on Route 14

achievements.name: Pokémania Runs in the Family
achievements.description: Show all 5 requested Pokémon to Bill's grandfather on Route 25.
achievements.callout_do: Show all 5 requested Pokémon to Bill's grandfather on Route 25.

achievements.name: The Lazy Song
achievements.description: Catch the Snorlax in Vermillion City and register it in the Pokédex.
achievements.callout_do: Catch the Snorlax east of Vermilion and register it.

achievements.name: Snorlax
achievements.note: wake it with the PokéFlute and catch it (save first)

achievements.description: Receive a Moon Stone after witnessing the Clefairy ritual at Mt. Moon Square.
achievements.callout_do: Receive a Moon Stone after the Clefairy ritual at Mt. Moon Square.

achievements.name: Elekid
achievements.note: breed Electabuzz × Ditto, then hatch Elekid

achievements.name: Pichu
achievements.note: breed Pikachu/Raichu × Ditto, then hatch Pichu

achievements.name: Cleffa

achievements.description: Have Daisy Oak comb your Pokémon in Pallet Town.
achievements.callout_do: Have Daisy Oak comb your Pokémon in Pallet Town.

achievements.name: Fateful Eye-to-Eye

achievements.name: Serenity Beyond the Storm
achievements.description: Catch Lugia and register it in the Pokédex without using a Master Ball.
achievements.callout_do: Catch Lugia without using a Master Ball.

achievements.callout_do: Catch Ho-Oh at the Tin Tower.

achievements.description: Catch Ho-Oh and register it in the Pokédex without using a Master Ball.
achievements.callout_do: Catch Ho-Oh without using a Master Ball.

achievements.callout_do: Defeat Lt. Surge in Vermilion City for the Thunder Badge.

achievements.name: Shock to the System
achievements.description: Defeat Lt. Surge in the Set battle style using only Johto Pokémon that do not exceed level 46 during battle and without using any Pack items - no external trade-only Pokémon or Legendaries other than the Beasts.
achievements.callout_do: Set-style, Johto-only, ≤ Lv. 46, no Pack items (Beasts allowed).
howto: Just reuse your League team — especially Piloswine. Low difficulty.

achievements.name: Thunder Badge
achievements.description: Defeat Lt. Surge with all 203 possible Pokémon in the Pokédex.

achievements.description: Defeat Sabrina in Saffron City and earn the Marsh Badge.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat Sabrina in Saffron City for the Marsh Badge.

achievements.description: Defeat Sabrina in the Set battle style using only Johto Pokémon that do not exceed level 48 during battle and without using any Pack items - no external trade-only Pokémon or Legendaries other than the Beasts.
achievements.callout_do: Set-style, Johto-only, ≤ Lv. 48, no Pack items (Beasts allowed).

achievements.name: Marsh Badge
achievements.description: Defeat Sabrina with all 203 possible Pokémon in the Pokédex.

achievements.name: The Third Water Wheel
achievements.description: Defeat Misty in Cerulean City and earn the Cascade Badge.
achievements.callout_do: Defeat Misty in Cerulean City for the Cascade Badge.

chapters.title: Chapter 0 — The Basics (read first)
chapters.intro: A Professor Oak Challenge means catching and evolving as many Pokémon as possible before each gym badge becomes required to proceed (or before you need an HM that needs a badge). The dex comes first — your gym challenge goes on hold. This guide fuses Mewlax's POC route with both RetroAchievements sets: the base game set and the Professor Oak Challenge subset. Facts come straight from those sources; nothing is invented.

items.text: Catch and evolve as many Pokémon as is possible until a gym badge is required to proceed or you need a HM move that requires a badge. Where gyms can be done in any order, always get the badge that unlocks the most Pokémon (this guide picks the order for you).

You cannot trade with other versions. Trade evolutions are off the table, you get no version exclusives, and you are stuck with the decisions you make such as your starter. In-game trades ARE allowed.
Optional: call yourself 'Oak' or 'Prof. Oak' for fun.

Move sets are slow. Consider keeping three-stage Pokémon unevolved a little longer so they learn better moves, and keep multiple attacking moves over status moves like Growl or Sand Attack — you will KO huge numbers of wild Pokémon.

Time of day matters. Crystal pushes this further than Gold/Silver — almost every route has Pokémon that appear only in one or two slots. Morning is 4am–9:59am, Day is 10am–5:59pm, Night is 6pm–3:59am.
This is a very grindy challenge; the part before Falkner alone can take tens of hours. It gets less grindy as the game proceeds.
This guide lists the Pokémon you can catch at the earliest opportunity plus required items — it is not a full walkthrough of optimal EXP routes. Consult Bulbapedia for deeper detail.

sections.heading: The Moon Stone savings plan (start this immediately)

items.text: When you leave New Bark Town after delivering the egg to Professor Elm, speak to your Mother before leaving so she starts saving your money. A quarter of every trainer's winnings goes to her automatically; you can also deposit manually at home to speed it up.

Thresholds and the item she buys: P900 → Super Potion; P4,000 → Repel; P7,000 → Super Potion; P10,000 → Charmander Doll; P15,000 → Moon Stone. Because her savings drop each time she buys, you need P18,270 deposited in total to reach the Moon Stone.
The phone call that delivers an item only triggers when you defeat a trainer while she is already at the threshold, or the winnings from that battle push her over it.

Many evolution stones and swarm catches come from Pokégear contacts calling you. You can force a call: go to New Bark Town, talk to your mother, and toggle daylight savings (alternate winter/summer time) to instantly prompt a contact to call.

To change the in-game date/time directly, on the title screen press Down + Select + B together to reach the password prompt. The password is based on your name, ID and money, so it stays the same as long as your money doesn't change — handy for save-reset tricks.

Celebi is obtainable only on the Virtual Console release (via the GS Ball). Cartridge players cannot get it. Final dex totals: 206 on cartridge, 207 on Virtual Console. VC-only steps are flagged inline.

chapters.title: Chapter 1 — Pre-Badge #1: Falkner
chapters.intro: Everything you can do BEFORE the first badge from Falkner — the most daunting stretch of the challenge. Thankfully there is no Jigglypuff on Route 46, so no Moon Stone is needed yet (but get your Mother saving from the very start). By the end you should have caught 46 Pokémon, including a fully evolved starter and Pidgeot.

text: Set the clock and start the game. Your Mother gives you a PokéGear.
Head to Professor Elm's lab. He asks you to visit Mr. Pokémon and lets you take one Pokémon partner.
Pick one starter — you can only pick one, and a POC forbids trading for the others:
Chikorita → Bayleef Lv. 16 → Meganium Lv. 32
Cyndaquil → Quilava Lv. 14 → Typhlosion Lv. 36
Totodile → Croconaw Lv. 18 → Feraligatr Lv. 30
Chikorita is the weakest start given all the bug/flying/poison early on; Totodile fully evolves earliest at Lv. 30.
Visit Mr. Pokémon's house (north past Cherrygrove), then return to New Bark Town to receive your Pokédex and Poké Balls — now the challenge truly begins.
Talk to Mom before leaving town to start the savings.

items.text: Consider leaving your starter unevolved a while so it learns moves faster for the long grind ahead.

text: Straight out of New Bark Town.
Pidgey, Sentret and Hoppip appear Morning/Day, Hoothoot at Night, and Rattata at all times.

rows.name: Potion
rows.where: East of the northeastern grass patch

text: Fully evolving Pidgey is one of the bigger grinds before Falkner.
Hoppip only knows Tackle (from Lv. 10) as an attacking move, even unevolved up to Skiploom/Jumpluff — train it patiently.
Through the northern gate of Route 29 you reach the lower section of Route 46.
Phanpy appears in the morning, Spearow Morning/Day, Geodude and Rattata at any time. (Jigglypuff is Gold/Silver-only here — not in Crystal, so no Moon Stone is needed yet.)
Phanpy has a high chance to flee each turn. If it wastes too many balls, come back later with a Gastly that knows Mean Look to trap it.
Graveler's evolution into Golem is a trade evolution, so Golem is NOT obtainable in a POC.
Pass through Cherrygrove City and head north to Route 30.
Ledyba appears in the morning; Caterpie and Weedle Morning/Day; Spinarak, Poliwag and Zubat at night.
Keep Zubat in your party at all times: Golbat evolves into Crobat via high happiness, and leveling on the route you caught it gives more friendship per level in Crystal.
Butterfree gives ridiculous EXP when flushed out of Headbutt trees later.
Poliwhirl needs a Water Stone for Poliwrath, which you cannot obtain yet — that comes later.
Head further north onto Route 31.
Bellsprout appears Morning and Night, Gastly at night (or get one at night in Sprout Tower). Catch a SECOND Bellsprout for an upcoming trade.

rows.name: Poké Ball
rows.where: Near Bug Catcher Wade

rows.name: Bitter Berry
rows.where: Berry tree near the sleeping man (daily respawn)

text: Weepinbell needs a Leaf Stone for Victreebel, handled later.
Haunter's evolution into Gengar is a trade evolution, so Gengar is NOT obtainable in a POC. A Haunter with Mean Look/Night Shade helps catch roaming legendaries later.
Instead of heading west to Violet City, enter Dark Cave (Route 31 entrance).
You won't get far, but you can stumble far enough to catch a Teddiursa in the morning and a super-rare 1% Dunsparce.
A Dunsparce with Rage is highly recommended for the Falkner Set challenge: it is Normal type and takes little damage.
Teddiursa is another fleeing Pokémon — bring something to trap it if needed.
It is not time for the gym yet.
In one of the town houses, someone wants to trade an Onix for your Bellsprout — definitely do this, as Onix can't be caught until much later.
Sprout Tower has a Gastly at night and the Flash HM at the top.
Head west out of Violet City.
Exclusive to Crystal, the grass here holds a Growlithe during Morning/Day.

rows.name: Schoolboy Alan
rows.note: Pokégear contact — later gives Fire Stones; team not sourced

items.text: Growlithe needs a Fire Stone for Arcanine, obtained later from a Pokégear contact (Schoolboy Alan).

text: Go through the gate house south of the Route 36 grass to reach the Ruins of Alph.
Solve the first tile puzzle to encounter Unown.
Catch one Unown now; you can collect all 26 forms later once every puzzle chamber is unlocked.

That's the end of catching for the first section. Grind your team up — Sprout Tower at night is great against Gastly, and Dark Cave / Route 31 / Ruins of Alph are good for the rest. The killers are the fully evolved starter, Pidgeot and Ursaring.

items.text: Back in New Bark Town, after the Mr. Pokémon errand, an officer at Professor Elm's lab asks for your rival's name (he raided the lab and stole a starter). Give it to register the achievement.
With all 46 Pokémon caught and your starter and Pidgey fully evolved, enter the Violet City Gym and challenge Falkner, who uses Flying types (Pidgey, Pidgeotto).

chapters.title: Chapter 2 — Pre-Badge #2: Bugsy
chapters.intro: With Falkner down, an aide hands you the Egg at the Violet City Pokémon Center — it hatches into Togepi. Work south through Route 32, the Old Rod fishing tour, Union Cave, Slowpoke Well and Ilex Forest, then start Headbutting trees. Target: 77 caught before Bugsy.

text: After beating Falkner, Professor Elm calls: an aide is at the Pokémon Center with a present — the Egg.
Keep it in your party so its happiness rises while you grind; it hatches into Togepi.

items.text: Show the hatched Togepi to Professor Elm to receive an Everstone.

text: Head south from Violet City (skip Ruins of Alph for now).
Ekans appears Morning/Day, Wooper only at Night.
The Pokémon Center on this route has a man who gives you the Old Rod.

rows.name: Old Rod
rows.where: Fishing Guru in the Route 32 Pokémon Center

rows.name: Great Ball
rows.where: Grass patch southwest of the pier

rows.name: TM05 Roar
rows.where: From a man on the ledge (needs Cut, return later)

rows.name: Fisher Ralph
rows.note: Pokégear contact — calls about Qwilfish swarms

text: With the Old Rod, backtrack to fish up new Pokémon.
Get Fisherman Ralph's number on Route 32 — if he calls about a Qwilfish outbreak, come back and catch one.
Catch a SECOND Krabby for a later trade.
Magikarp can't battle until it learns Tackle — train it once it can.
Qwilfish needs the Super Rod and a Ralph swarm call. Toggle daylight savings with Mom to force the call.
Enter Union Cave at the south end of Route 32.
Sandshrew appears Morning/Day.
The cave is bigger than it looks but you can't fully explore it yet; the far side leads to Azalea Town.

rows.name: Potion
rows.where: 1F, northwest corner

rows.name: Great Ball
rows.where: 1F, southeast near a trainer

rows.name: Awakening
rows.where: 1F, near the Route 33 exit

rows.name: TM39 Swift
rows.where: B1F, west of the ladder

text: Go to Kurt's house in Azalea; he leaves for the Slowpoke Well.
Follow and defeat the Rocket grunt leading the raid, then catch a Slowpoke.
Afterward Kurt makes special balls from Apricorns.

items.text: Slowpoke's other evolution, Slowking, is a trade evolution — not obtainable in a POC.

text: Leave Azalea west (defeat your rival first) into Ilex Forest.
Grab the Cut HM here.
Paras appears any time (5%); Psyduck, Oddish and Venonat at Night.

rows.name: HM01 Cut
rows.where: From the Farfetch'd-herding boy event

rows.name: TM02 Headbutt
rows.where: Ilex Forest

rows.name: Revive
rows.where: Ilex Forest

rows.name: Ether
rows.where: Ilex Forest

items.text: Gloom needs a Leaf Stone or Sun Stone later for Vileplume/Bellossom.

text: Even blocked off without Cut, you can Headbutt trees once Slowpoke learns Headbutt.
Try a tree and see what falls out: Pineco in Ilex Forest, Aipom and Heracross in Azalea Town, Exeggcute back on Route 32.
Headbutt odds depend on the tree, not the time of day — rarer mon like Heracross only appear from the 'low chance' trees, so keep trying different trees.
Exeggcute needs a Leaf Stone later for Exeggutor.
Heracross gives huge EXP and is a great team member; not required for the Hive milestone but part of the dex.

items.text: With 77 Pokémon registered, enter the Azalea Town Gym and challenge Bugsy, who uses Bug types.

rows.name: Leader Bugsy
rows.team: Metapod Lv. 14, Kakuna Lv. 14, Scyther Lv. 16

chapters.title: Chapter 3 — Pre-Badge #3: Whitney
chapters.intro: Make sure you secure the Moon Stone from Mom this section (deposit up to P18,270). With Cut you push through Ilex Forest to Goldenrod City. Route 34, the Odd Egg, the Game Corner, the Bug-Catching Contest and a stash of evolution stones await. Target: 109 caught before Whitney.

text: Several new Pokémon plus the Day-Care.
Snubbull appears Morning/Day, Drowzee at Night; everything else any time.
Catch a SECOND Abra for an upcoming trade and get Picnicker Gina's number (she gives Leaf Stones).

rows.name: TM12 Sweet Scent
rows.where: From the woman at the gate to Ilex Forest

rows.name: Nugget
rows.where: Fenced area west of Picnicker Gina (needs Surf, Crystal only)

rows.name: Rare Candy
rows.where: Hidden in the fenced area (needs Surf)

rows.name: Picnicker Gina
rows.note: Pokégear contact — calls about Leaf Stones

rows.name: Youngster Samuel
rows.team: Rattata Lv. 7, Spearow Lv. 8 ×2, Sandshrew Lv. 10

items.text: Kadabra's evolution into Alakazam is a trade evolution — not obtainable in a POC.

text: Talk to the old man outside the Day-Care for the Odd Egg.
SAVE BEFORE TALKING TO HIM — the species is fixed when received. It can be Pichu, Cleffa, Igglybuff, Tyrogue, Smoochum, Elekid or Magby.
Soft-reset until you get Tyrogue (it nets four Pokémon now via the Hitmon line).
With the exception of Igglybuff, the other Odd Egg species aren't available yet — Tyrogue gives the most dex progress, and there's a high shiny chance.
Tyrogue's evolution depends on its stats at Lv. 20: Defense > Attack → Hitmonchan, Attack > Defense → Hitmonlee, Attack = Defense → Hitmontop.

items.text: Breed your Jigglypuff with Ditto at the Day-Care to produce Igglybuff.

sections.heading: 3.3 · Goldenrod City — bike, Underground & Game Corner

text: The biggest city in Johto. Grab the bike.
The Underground has a Coin Case for the Game Corner, and you can trade your spare Abra for a Machop.
Crystal's Game Corner offers Cubone and Wobbuffet.

items.text: Machoke's evolution into Machamp is a trade evolution — not obtainable in a POC.

text: North of Goldenrod, Route 35 holds an ultra-rare 1% Yanma; Growlithe appears Morning/Day (you need it for the later Fire Stone evolution).
Through the north gate is National Park: Nidoran♂/♀ Morning/Day, Sunkern in the daytime.
On Tue/Thu/Sat the Bug-Catching Contest runs in National Park.

items.text: National Park's tall-grass slots are unusual in that nearly every mon is split by time of day — re-check the table for Morning vs Day vs Night. Sunkern only appears in the Day slot.

rows.name: TM04 Rollout
rows.where: Route 35, southern grass patch

rows.name: Paralyze Heal
rows.where: National Park, behind the fence near the east gate

rows.name: TM28 Dig
rows.where: National Park, behind the fence in the southwest

rows.name: Full Heal
rows.where: National Park, hidden between two flowers near the south gate

text: Now evolve your stone Pokémon.
Mom's Moon Stone evolves one of Jigglypuff / Nidorina / Nidorino; two Sun Stones come from winning the Bug Contest.
Leaf and Fire Stones arrive via Gina and Schoolboy Alan's PokéGear calls (force them with the daylight-savings trick).
You get three Moon Stones across Parts 3 and 5 — spread them across Wigglytuff, Nidoking and Nidoqueen.
Catch/raise a second Gloom so you can get BOTH Bellossom (Sun Stone) and Vileplume (Leaf Stone).

items.text: With 109 Pokémon registered, challenge Whitney in the Goldenrod City Gym for the Plain Badge.

chapters.title: Chapter 4 — Pre-Badge #4: Morty
chapters.intro: Catch Sudowoodo, sweep Routes 37–43 and Olivine, pick up the Good Rod, then — yes, this early — chase down and catch the roaming beasts Raikou and Entei. Target: 142 caught before Morty.

text: After Whitney, return to Route 36 and talk to the lady by the 'tree'. She sends you to the Goldenrod flower shop for the SquirtBottle.
Bring it back and SAVE before using it on the tree — this is your only Sudowoodo (a one-off static battle, not a wild slot). The man on its right then gives you the Rock Smash TM.

rows.name: TM08 Rock Smash
rows.where: From the man right of the Sudowoodo tree (after the battle)

items.text: Route 37 has a single new catch worth stopping for, Stantler, at Night only. Vulpix is also available here in the daytime.

rows.name: Red / Blu / Blk Apricorns
rows.where: From the three Apricorn trees on the route

items.text: Reach Ecruteak and enter the Pokémon Center; Bill heads back to Goldenrod. Follow him and he gives you an Eevee. Breed it five times — use the babies for Espeon/Umbreon (higher base happiness).

Take on the Kimono Girls for the Surf HM, then visit the Burned Tower. Koffing lurks on both floors. In the basement you disturb the three legendary beasts — Raikou and Entei begin roaming; Suicune appears in set locations later.

rows.name: HP Up
rows.where: 1F, behind a breakable rock (needs Rock Smash)

rows.name: TM20 Endure
rows.where: B1F

rows.name: Ultra Ball
rows.where: 1F (hidden)

items.text: West to Route 38. Magnemite any time, Tauros & Miltank Morning/Day, Meowth at Night. Beat Lass Dana and get her number for Thunder Stones.

rows.name: Lass Dana
rows.note: Pokégear contact — calls about Thunder Stones

items.text: On Route 39 between Ecruteak and Olivine, stop at Moomoo Farm and help nurse the sick Miltank, Moomoo, back to health (it then sells Moomoo Milk and gives you TM13 Snore).
Reach the lighthouse top and talk to Jasmine to start Amphy's errand (not done yet). Trade your spare Krabby for a Voltorb, grab the Strength HM, then smash rocks on Route 40 for a chance at Shuckle.

rows.name: HM04 Strength
rows.where: From the Sailor in the Olivine Café

sections.heading: 4.6 · Good Rod fishing & the Water Stone

text: Pick up the Good Rod from the Fishing Guru in Olivine for more catches, then journey to Route 42 / Mt. Mortar. Defeat Fisherman Tully past the mountain for the final stone contact — Water Stones now evolve several Pokémon.
Shellder and Chinchou bite on the Good Rod at the Olivine harbor and in New Bark Town; Corsola and Staryu bite in Olivine City itself.

rows.name: Good Rod
rows.where: From the Fishing Guru north of the Pokémon Center

items.text: Marill also appears during a Mt. Mortar swarm at higher rates; check the Pokégear for an active outbreak.
Rest in Mahogany Town, then head north to Route 43 for a Farfetch'd (Morning/Day). Keep going to the Lake of Rage (nothing to do there yet).

rows.name: Camper Spencer
rows.team: Sandshrew Lv. 17, Sandslash Lv. 17, Zubat Lv. 19

items.text: Time to catch TWO legendaries early. Raikou and Entei roam Johto, moving each time you change area. Hunt from Ecruteak / Route 36 / Route 37 (no gates). Spray a Repel (lead ≤ Lv. 40), chip their HP across encounters, trap with Mean Look, status them, and use Ultra/Great Balls (the Fast Ball is bugged). Save between attempts.
As you cross Route 42 during all this, you'll witness Suicune in a set location — part of the scripted sequence that lets you catch it later.

chapters.title: Chapter 5 — Pre-Badge #5: Pryce
chapters.intro: Surf opens new ground: Tohjo Falls, the depths of Union Cave and the Ruins of Alph, Route 41 and Cianwood. Catch the red Gyarados, clear the Mahogany Rocket Hideout, then pick Pryce (he unlocks Whirlpool). Target: 150 caught.

text: Surf east from New Bark to Route 27 and enter Tohjo Falls to reach a Moon Stone.

In Union Cave's basement, Surf across to the grassy outside of the Ruins of Alph for Natu and Smeargle (Smeargle is rare — don't KO it). On a Friday a Lapras appears on Union Cave B2F (Surf the southernmost pond).
The Aerodactyl puzzle room hides a third Moon Stone (use Flash on the wall writing).

rows.method: Union Cave B2F Surf — Fridays only, Lv. 20

rows.name: Moon Stone
rows.where: Tohjo Falls (reached by Surf from Route 27)

rows.name: Moon Stone
rows.where: Ruins of Alph Aerodactyl puzzle room (Flash the wall writing)

items.text: Surf south from Olivine over Route 40 onto Route 41 (borders Cianwood) and Surf up a Mantine. At Cianwood, grab Amphy's medicine at the pharmacy; head north to see Suicune, where Eusine challenges you — required to meet Suicune later. Mania here lends you the Shuckle 'Shuckie'.

rows.name: Eusine
rows.team: Drowzee Lv. 23, Haunter Lv. 23, Electrode Lv. 25

items.text: As the Suicune sequence continues you'll also witness it on Route 36.

sections.heading: 5.3 · Lake of Rage & the Mahogany Rocket Hideout

items.text: Surf to the red Gyarados (Lv. 30) at the Lake of Rage. After catching/defeating it, Lance teams up with you to raid the Rocket Hideout in Mahogany. Inside you get the Whirlpool HM — also grab the Thief TM for a Moon Stone later. The Fishing Guru by the lake rewards a record Magikarp.

rows.name: HM06 Whirlpool
rows.where: Mahogany Rocket Hideout (story event with Lance)

rows.name: TM46 Thief
rows.where: Mahogany Rocket Hideout

items.text: Three gyms are open now; Pryce is the pick because the Glacier Badge lets you use Whirlpool (and unlocks more Pokémon).

rows.name: Leader Pryce
rows.team: Seel Lv. 27, Dewgong Lv. 29, Piloswine Lv. 31

chapters.title: Chapter 6 — Pre-Badges #6 & #7: Jasmine & Chuck
chapters.intro: A very short stretch: Whirlpool opens the Whirl Islands for Seel and Horsea, then cure Amphy and clear both Chuck and Jasmine back to back. Target: 154 caught.

text: With Whirlpool, enter the Whirl Islands off the Olivine–Cianwood surf route. Seel appears in the cave grass Morning/Day (not Night); Horsea is found while Surfing on the inner pools.
Seadra also shows up Surfing in the inner cave and on the Super Rod, so you can catch one outright if you'd rather not grind Horsea to Lv. 32.

items.text: Krabby is the bulk of the cave grass and rises to a 60% Night rate; Seel and Zubat occupy the daytime slots. The Surf Horsea rate climbs from the entrance pools (30%) toward the inner cave.
Seadra's evolution into Kingdra is a trade evolution — not obtainable in a POC.
Zubat's Night cave rate could not be sourced reliably (Bulbapedia shows no Night slot; Serebii lists 30%) — Morning/Day 30% is agreed, so the Night value is left blank.
items.missing_info: Whirl Islands Zubat cave Night rate: Bulbapedia (0%/absent) vs Serebii (30%) disagree — left blank.

text: Deliver the SecretPotion to the top of the Olivine Lighthouse to cure Amphy — this opens Jasmine's gym.
Defeat Chuck for the Storm Badge in Cianwood, then Jasmine for the Mineral Badge in Olivine, back to back.

rows.name: Leader Chuck
rows.note: carries a Full Heal — Flying/Psychic hits both

rows.name: Leader Jasmine
rows.note: carries a Hyper Potion — Ground ignores the Magnemite

chapters.title: Chapter 7 — Pre-Badge #8: Clair
chapters.intro: Stop Team Rocket's Radio Tower takeover in Goldenrod, then use the Clear Bell to catch Suicune at the Tin Tower. Cross Route 44 and the Ice Path to Blackthorn, fish two Dratini on Route 45, then beat Clair. Target: 170.

items.text: After the 7th badge Professor Elm calls you to the Goldenrod Radio Tower. Clear it of Team Rocket; you can train against the grunts.

Clearing the Radio Tower earns the Clear Bell. Go to the Tin Tower in Ecruteak, beat the sages, and follow the trail. SAVE BEFORE ENTERING — if you fail, Suicune is gone forever. Unlike the others it will NOT flee. It knows Rain Dance, Bubblebeam, Leer and Gust; bring a resist, drop its HP to red and inflict sleep/paralysis, then use Ultra Balls.

text: With 7 badges the man blocking eastern Mahogany is gone. Route 44's grass sits on an island mid-lake — Surf across to reach it.
Lickitung appears Morning/Day only; Poliwag/Poliwhirl replace it at Night. Tangela, Bellsprout and Weepinbell appear any time.

text: Blackthorn has a required in-game trade: a female Dragonair for a Dodrio (it arrives holding a Smoke Ball; only after badge 8).
South on Route 45 (bring Fly for the ledges): Gligar any time, Skarmory Morning/Day; fish the pond at the south end for Dratini (Good/Super Rod) — get two, one female for the trade.
Then breed Dodrio→Doduo and Jynx→Smoochum at the Day-Care.

items.text: With 170 Pokémon registered, challenge Clair in the Blackthorn City Gym. She awards TM24 DragonBreath with the Rising Badge (the Dragon's Den test follows before the badge is official).

chapters.title: Chapter 8 — Pre-Elite Four
chapters.intro: Clear the Dragon's Den trial for badge 8 and Waterfall, grab the Master Ball from Elm, then cross into Route 27 and Victory Road catching the last Pokémon before challenging the Elite Four. Target: 174 caught.

text: After Clair, complete the trial in the Dragon's Den for your final badge and Waterfall.
Answer every question of the Master's quiz correctly on the FIRST attempt to receive a Dratini with ExtremeSpeed.
Then see Prof. Elm in New Bark Town for the Master Ball.
Surf east of New Bark and cross Tohjo Falls via Waterfall on the way to Route 27.
The cave's wild list is all Pokémon you already have, but the small western ledge holds a Moon Stone (needs Surf).

rows.name: Moon Stone
rows.where: Small piece of land at the western end of the cave (needs Surf)

text: The twin routes east of Tohjo Falls into Kanto's edge.
Catch a Ponyta — it appears Morning/Day on both routes (20% on Route 26, 5% on Route 27).
Noctowl, Quagsire and Raticate fill the Night slots.

rows.name: TM22 SolarBeam
rows.where: Isolated land south of the Route 27 bridge (needs Surf + Whirlpool)

rows.name: Rare Candy
rows.where: Tiny area southeast of Tohjo Falls (needs Surf)

rows.name: TM37 Sandstorm
rows.where: From the woman in the house east of Tohjo Falls, at high friendship

rows.name: Ice Berry
rows.where: North of the Route 26 rest stop (daily)

text: The final cave before the Indigo Plateau, full of Rock/Ground mons.
Catch Rhyhorn Morning/Day (30%), or hunt the 5% wild Rhydon directly. Onix, Graveler and Golbat round out the floors.

items.text: At the Indigo Plateau your rival challenges you in an optional battle before the League. Then take on the Elite Four and Champion Lance.

chapters.title: Chapter 9 — Conquering Kanto
chapters.intro: As Champion you sail to Kanto. Most remaining Pokémon can be caught before a single Kanto badge — restore the Power Plant, upgrade the radio card, sweep the region, and catch Lugia and Ho-Oh. (Virtual Console players can also pick up Celebi.) Target: 203 caught.

items.text: VC ONLY: collect the GS Ball from the Goldenrod Pokémon Center upstairs, give it to Kurt in Azalea, return next day, then interact with the Ilex Forest shrine to battle a Lv. 30 Celebi (it Recovers — chip its HP and status it like Suicune). Cartridge players cannot get Celebi.

Sail the S.S. Aqua from Olivine to Vermilion. Go north to the Route 10 Power Plant area: catch an Electabuzz in the grass, then learn a part was stolen. Chase the Rocket through the Cerulean Gym, recover the part from Nugget Bridge, and return it to the Power Plant.

sections.heading: 9.3 · Rock Tunnel, the radio card & Magnet Train

items.text: South to Rock Tunnel: catch Kangaskhan (lower floors) and find wild Cubone/Marowak holding Thick Clubs. Exit south to Lavender Town's Radio Tower for the radio expansion card (PokéFlute). In Saffron, the Copycat wants her doll — fetch it from the Vermilion Pokémon Fan Club to get the Magnet Train pass.

From Saffron head toward Celadon. Route 7 at Night has Houndour and Murkrow. In Celadon, Surf the small pool for Grimer (10% Muk). The Game Corner sells Porygon (5,555 coins) and Larvitar (8,888). Prove to Eusine in the Celadon Mart that you've caught Raikou/Entei/Suicune for the Rainbow Wing. Slugma appears on Route 16 in the daytime.

On Route 15 catch the 1% Chansey (Lv. 25 — repel-trick with a Lv. 24 lead helps). Breed it so you keep one, then trade a Chansey on Route 14 for Aerodactyl. Bill's grandfather on Route 25 rewards showing him specific Pokémon.

Wake the Snorlax east of Vermilion with the PokéFlute (save first) to open Diglett's Cave for Diglett. Emerge on Route 2 for Pikachu, then Pewter (Silver Wing from the old man) and Mt. Moon for Clefairy and a Moon Stone (Monday-night ritual needs Rock Smash). Surf at Pallet to Route 21 for Mr. Mime.
Back at the Johto Day-Care, breed your Kanto catches for their babies. Daisy Oak in Pallet will comb a Pokémon for you.

With the Rainbow Wing and Silver Wing, catch BOTH Lugia (deep in the Whirl Islands) and Ho-Oh (top of the Tin Tower), each at Lv. 60. Heavy Balls help on Lugia; use your Master Ball on one and catch the other normally.

chapters.title: Chapter 10 — The Kanto Gym Circuit
chapters.intro: A POC catches everything before Kanto badges, so this chapter collects the eight Kanto gym wins and their Set-mode challenges in one place. Each badge is also a POC checkpoint at 203 Pokémon. Order follows the community route: Surge → Sabrina → Misty → Erika → Janine → Brock → Blaine → Blue.

chapters.title: Chapter 11 — Mt. Silver & Red
chapters.intro: With all 16 badges, Professor Oak opens Mt. Silver. Catch the last two wild Pokémon in Silver Cave, then face the former Champion, Red, at the summit. This completes the challenge: 206 caught (207 on VC with Celebi).

text: From the Pokémon League gates, exit west to Route 28 and the Pokémon Center at the foot of Mt. Silver, then enter Silver Cave (Flash lights the first room).
On 1F, Magmar appears Morning/Day — catch one to breed for Magby.
Climb to 2F: Misdreavus shows up only at Night (5% on 2F; up to 30% deeper in the Chambers). These are the last two wild Pokémon you need.

items.text: Climb to the top of Mt. Silver to battle Red — the highest-level Trainer in the game. His team is mid-to-low 70s with a Lv. 81 Pikachu, and he carries two Full Restores; bring your best six.

rows.name: Pokémon Trainer Red
rows.team: Pikachu Lv. 81, Snorlax Lv. 75, Venusaur Lv. 77, Charizard Lv. 77, Blastoise Lv. 77, Espeon Lv. 73

chapters.title: Roundup — Collectible & Challenge Checklist
chapters.intro: These achievements span the whole game — work them in alongside the route. They cover full-region trainer/item sweeps, the complete Unown set, every trade and gift, the fishing rods, the Battle Tower, and the grand 'Gotta Catch 'Em All' capstone.
